title: BB-088: Editorial trust and pre-bunking surfaces
description:
Expansion bead BB-088 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (editorial trust & pre-bunking surfaces: protection framed as rigor, never defensiveness).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("People are going to hate it. People are going to try to discredit the information. People are going to try to mock it... I need you to protect it without saying that is what we are doing explicitly")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-053, BB-055, BB-069, BB-086

Why: the application will face coordinated attempts to discredit it. The winning posture is radical transparency experienced as rigor and reader empowerment — never a defensive crouch. The mechanisms exist (confidence + disputes BB-053, corrections BB-055, link integrity BB-083, fact records BB-086) but the PUBLIC-FACING trust layer that converts "someone found an error" into evidence of rigor, and that inoculates readers against bad-faith framing before they meet it, does not. Pre-bunking (inoculation) research is explicit that it works precisely because it is "educational, non-judgmental, and non-accusatory" — which is exactly the owner's ask to protect the work without ever announcing that's what we're doing. The whole bead is that principle: teach readers how records get attacked so they can verify for themselves; never argue with critics in copy.

Deliver:
- Methodology page ("How we work"): mission & scope; definitions & inclusion criteria (the load-bearing section — EJI's definitional precision is what defuses "your numbers don't match theirs"); source hierarchy (what counts as primary); verification + triangulation process; known limitations & gaps stated plainly (this section IS the pre-bunk); update cadence; how to report an error; funding/independence; masthead. Adopt all 8 Trust Project practices and emit the CC-BY-SA schema vocabulary they contributed, but never use their trademarked name or badge. Publish the five IFCN commitments verbatim as "aligned with" (the badge requires paid signatory status; the text is public).
- Corrections policy + public errata log: four-way change taxonomy (correction | clarification | update | editor's note — PolitiFact); never-silent, appended, timestamped notes on the item itself (NPR); a single reverse-chronological errata page (NPR/ProPublica pattern) ALSO exposed as an RSS/JSON feed; AP tone standard — errors fixed "fully, quickly, and ungrudgingly." Wire to BB-055 and the BB-086 revision log; emit schema.org/correction on corrected facts (an early-adopter trust signal; <1,000 domains use it).
- Per-fact provenance display + revision history (MediaWiki pattern): status + confidence with a tooltip linking to the methodology definitions; full sources list rendered from CSL-JSON with archived-copy link + access date + supporting excerpt per source (so a reader can replicate the work); "Our basis" prose note; counter-claims addressed inline. "Updated <date> — see what changed" -> per-revision diff view with mandatory edit summaries, corrections visually distinguished from routine updates. Git-backed content storage gives this nearly free and yields an independently verifiable audit trail a hostile reader can clone.
- Pre-bunking copy system (framed only as reader empowerment; voice owned by BB-069):
  - a site-wide "How to read this record" component (forewarn + technique micro-dose): "Historical records get challenged in predictable ways — quoting a source out of context, demanding a single 'official' document for events that were deliberately never documented, or attacking the messenger instead of the evidence. Here's how this record is built so you can check it yourself."
  - confidence labels that pre-empt the gotcha: never bare "disputed" — "Corroborated: three independent primary sources agree on the date; contemporary accounts differ on the crowd size, and we show both."
  - a per-fact "Common misreadings" section powered by BB-086 counterClaims[]: "You may see this described as X. That reading relies on [technique]; the primary record shows Y (source, page, archived copy)."
  - methodology copy that inoculates against the error-hunt: "An archive of thousands of sourced facts will contain errors. What matters is what happens next: every correction is logged publicly, timestamped, and preserved in the record's history — nothing is silently edited."
  - name techniques, never people or groups ("this framing," "this tactic" — never "critics"/"deniers"); the non-accusatory tone is what makes inoculation land across the whole spectrum.
- Schema.org trust markup: site-level NewsMediaOrganization with correctionsPolicy / verificationFactCheckingPolicy / ethicsPolicy / ownershipFundingInfo / masthead / actionableFeedbackPolicy; per-page publishingPrinciples.
- Myths/misconceptions surface: the ONE place ClaimReview markup is emitted (reviews of genuinely circulating third-party claims, per Google eligibility — attributed to a distinct external origin, one per page); optionally submitted to the free Fact Check Tools API for Explorer/research distribution, with ZERO SEO expectation (Google retired ClaimReview snippets June 2025 — treat as distribution, never infrastructure).

Design: inoculation = forewarning + preemptive refutation carrying a weakened micro-dose of the manipulation; technique-based prebunks scale better than claim-by-claim debunks; effects decay, so the trust chrome (status chip, source count, "updated — see what changed") appears on EVERY surface — map popup, place page, embed — as an ambient booster, not a buried about-page. Sources (all free): Cambridge/Jigsaw A Practical Guide to Prebunking Misinformation; Trust Project 8 indicators; IFCN five commitments; methodology exemplars EJI / Mapping Police Violence / ProPublica / Marshall Project; corrections exemplars NPR / ProPublica / AP.

Notes: This is the owner's "protect it without saying explicitly that's what we're doing" — the entire surface reads as rigor and empowerment. Directly answers the red-team's cherry-picked-screenshot / nitpick-one-error / discrediting-campaign class: the error-hunt is converted into the trust signal, and per-revision permalinks (BB-086) refute out-of-context screenshots with the live cited version. Mostly one-time content + small reusable UI components — write once, runs forever.
design: Inoculation = forewarning + preemptive refutation with a weakened micro-dose; technique-based prebunks scale better than claim-by-claim; works because it is educational, non-judgmental, non-accusatory (the owner's protect-without-saying-so). Effects decay, so trust chrome (status chip, source count, updated-see-what-changed) is an ambient booster on every surface incl. map popups and embeds. Free sources: Cambridge/Jigsaw Prebunking Guide, Trust Project 8 indicators, IFCN commitments, EJI/MPV/ProPublica/Marshall methodology exemplars, NPR/ProPublica/AP corrections exemplars.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Methodology page shipped (mission/scope, definitions & inclusion criteria, source hierarchy, verification+triangulation, known limitations, update cadence, report-an-error, funding/independence, masthead); 8 Trust Project practices adopted and their CC-BY-SA schema vocabulary emitted without using the trademarked name/badge; IFCN five commitments published as aligned-with. 2. Corrections policy + a single reverse-chronological errata page also exposed as RSS/JSON; four-way change taxonomy; never-silent timestamped notes on the item; schema.org/correction emitted on corrected facts. 3. Per-fact provenance display (status+confidence w/ definitions tooltip, sources list with archived-copy link + access date + excerpt, Our-basis note, counter-claims inline) + MediaWiki-style revision history with per-revision diffs and mandatory edit summaries. 4. Pre-bunking copy system live (site-wide How-to-read-this-record component, gotcha-pre-empting confidence labels, per-fact Common-misreadings from BB-086 counterClaims, error-hunt inoculation copy) - non-accusatory, names techniques never people. 5. Site-level NewsMediaOrganization trust markup + per-page publishingPrinciples. 6. Myths surface is the only ClaimReview emitter, optionally fed to the free Fact Check Tools API with zero SEO expectation.
notes: The owner's protect-it-without-saying-so, made concrete. Converts the error-hunt into the trust signal; per-revision permalinks (BB-086) refute out-of-context screenshots with the live cited version. Answers red-team cherry-picked-screenshot/nitpick-one-error/discrediting class. Mostly one-time content + small reusable components - write once, runs forever.

title: BB-085: Operator contribution lane: Claude sessions, CLI, and admin quick-add
description:
Expansion bead BB-085 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (operator contribution lane: Claude sessions, CLI, and admin quick-add).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("my periodic working on the project via claude sessions or something else to directly add information, or via the admin surfaces... make sure the search and agents are thorough with some really really really solid skills and packages")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-029, BB-044, BB-056

Why: the owner's working model is periodic deep sessions (Claude Code or admin console) between automated runs. Today there is no first-class path for an operator to add a source, lead, or claim without hand-crafting Firestore writes — which would bypass the pipeline. The operator lane makes direct contribution fast WITHOUT bypassing any gate: same quarantine, same research cases, same promotion rules, streamlined ergonomics.

Deliver:
- Operator ingestion CLI (repo script/package): submit a lead (URL/description/location/era), register a source, attach evidence to a case, or bulk-import from CSV/markdown notes — each lands in the standard quarantine/research-case pipeline stamped with operator identity and session ID; full audit; publishing still requires the promotion gates (proposer != approver holds — the operator proposes, publication approval is a distinct fresh-auth action).
- Admin console quick-add (BB-056 surface extension): the same operations as UI forms — paste a URL, the system fetches through BB-030 safety, captures via Wayback, pre-fills citation metadata, and opens a draft research case.
- Claude-session research workflow as project skills (.claude/skills/): well-crafted skills wrapping the real packages —
  - research-intake: take a URL/topic from the owner, run it through capture + citation scaffolding + draft-case creation via the CLI;
  - triage-graylist: walk parked weak-signal candidates, surface corroboration options, prepare accept/reject proposals;
  - discovery-run: launch a bounded adapter campaign and summarize yield;
  - case-drafting: assemble claims/evidence/confidence into a review-ready research case.
  Skills call the same TypeScript/Python packages the workers use (single implementation, no parallel logic in prompts); each skill documents exactly which invariants it cannot cross (no publish, no quarantine bypass).
- Session ergonomics: a docs/runbooks/operator-session.md runbook (start here, common tasks, end-of-session checklist including bd + git close protocol) so any future session — human or agent — is productive in minutes.
- Solid-packages rule (acceptance-level): pipeline capabilities land as tested package code (@black-book/* or the Python worker packages), and skills/automation are thin callers — never business logic living only in prompts or one-off scripts.
design: The owner works in periodic deep sessions (Claude Code or admin console) between automated runs. Making direct contribution fast WITHOUT bypassing gates keeps the single pipeline honest: same quarantine, same cases, same promotion rules, better ergonomics. Skills call the same code the workers run - single implementation.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Operator ingestion CLI: submit lead, register source, attach evidence, bulk-import CSV/markdown - all landing in the standard quarantine/research-case pipeline stamped with operator identity + session ID; a test proves publishing still requires the distinct promotion approval (proposer is not approver). 2. Admin console quick-add: paste a URL -> BB-030-safe fetch -> Wayback capture -> prefilled citation -> draft research case. 3. Project skills committed (.claude/skills/): research-intake, triage-graylist, discovery-run, case-drafting - each a thin caller over tested package code, each documenting the invariants it cannot cross. 4. docs/runbooks/operator-session.md committed (start-here, common tasks, end-of-session checklist incl. bd + git close protocol). 5. Solid-packages rule enforced in review: pipeline capabilities live in tested @black-book/* or Python worker packages; skills and automation are thin callers; no business logic that exists only in prompts or one-off scripts.
notes: Owner brief 2026-07-17: periodic working via claude sessions or admin surfaces to directly add information; agents thorough with really solid skills and packages.

title: BB-084: Self-updating operations: scheduled automation backbone
description:
Expansion bead BB-084 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (self-updating operations: the scheduled automation backbone).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("I need a lot of this to be self updating and will rely a lot on the automated processes that run")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-018, BB-035, BB-037

Why: the product must stay alive between owner sessions. Many beads now assume recurring jobs (deletion-sync, link checks, discovery campaigns, recalibration, dataset refreshes) but no bead owns the scheduling backbone. ADR-007 already picked the mechanism (Cloud Scheduler -> Cloud Tasks -> Cloud Run jobs/workers); this bead builds the framework those jobs plug into.

Deliver:
- Scheduled-job registry (versioned config, BB-037 registry discipline): every recurring job declares owner, cadence, budget, timeout, idempotency key scheme, kill switch, and target worker. No ad-hoc crons.
- The initial job roster:
  - discovery campaigns per adapter on per-source cadences (Wikimedia/federal weekly-monthly; RSS hourly-daily; Internet Archive/DPLA weekly; web-search campaigns budget-gated) (BB-039/073/075)
  - Reddit deletion-sync (contractual 48h window — BB-074/077)
  - citation link-health sweeps (BB-083)
  - external dataset refresh checks: FBI hate-crime annual, Tougaloo/Mapping Inequality revisions (BB-082)
  - relevance/confidence recalibration report (BB-081)
  - source drift + adapter run-health checks (BB-037), gold-corpus regression on engine changes (BB-047)
  - backup verification + restore-drill scheduling (BB-020/061), cost/budget report (BB-033)
- Run-health and missed-run alerting: every job emits run records (the research worker's run-health pattern generalized); a job that misses N consecutive runs or exceeds budget raises an operator alert (BB-034) — silence is a failure mode, not a default.
- Invariants hold under automation: all jobs run in the internal environment with least-privilege SAs; nothing scheduled can publish (assertDiscoveryCannotPublish and promotion gates stay binding) — automation PROPOSES (candidates, reports, flags); the owner or reviewers DISPOSE via the admin console or operator lane (BB-085). The only fully automatic public-facing effects are mechanical and reversible: link repairs to archived copies (BB-083) and release-coupled rebuilds (BB-070 map source, search index) — each with its own kill switch.
- Every automated write goes through the BB-018 audit/outbox path with a job-run correlation ID, so any automated change is traceable to the exact run that made it.
design: ADR-007 already picked the mechanism (Cloud Scheduler -> Cloud Tasks -> Cloud Run jobs); this bead builds the framework jobs plug into. Generalizes the research worker run-health pattern. Automation proposes; the owner/reviewers dispose via admin console or the BB-085 operator lane.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Scheduled-job registry (versioned config): every recurring job declares owner, cadence, budget, timeout, idempotency scheme, kill switch, target worker; no unregistered recurring jobs. 2. Initial roster registered: per-adapter discovery campaigns, Reddit deletion-sync, citation link-health sweeps, external dataset refresh checks (FBI annual, Tougaloo/Mapping Inequality revisions), recalibration report, source drift + run-health checks, gold-corpus regression, backup verification + drill scheduling, cost/budget report. 3. Run records + missed-run and over-budget operator alerts, tested (silence is a failure mode). 4. Publish invariants proven under automation: assertDiscoveryCannotPublish and promotion gates hold for every scheduled job; the only fully-automatic public-facing effects are mechanical and reversible (link repairs, release-coupled rebuilds), each with a kill switch. 5. Every automated write carries a job-run correlation ID through the BB-018 audit/outbox path.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: scheduling framework (packages/config/src/scheduled-jobs/) implementing ADR-007's Cloud Scheduler -> Cloud Tasks -> Cloud Run Jobs mechanism as a versioned, fail-closed registry (mirrors BB-037/BB-038's registry pattern exactly) — every job declares owner, cadence, budget, timeout, idempotency scheme, kill-switch reference, and target worker; unregistered job ids are rejected.

15-entry roster (roster.ts): 4 REAL job bodies wired to already-shipped, tested code — source-drift-run-health-check (-> @black-book/domain evaluateRunHealth + new workers/research/.../scheduled_jobs.py), gold-corpus-regression (-> @black-book/testing evaluateCorpus, BB-047), backup-verification-daily and restore-drill-quarterly (-> real subprocess calls into scripts/backup-restore/, BB-020) — plus 11 documented stubs (each carrying implementationOwnerBead) for capabilities owned by not-yet-built beads: 4 discovery-campaign cadence classes (BB-073/075), reddit-deletion-sync (BB-074/077), citation-link-health-sweep (BB-083), 2 external-dataset-refresh checks (BB-082), relevance-confidence-recalibration-report (BB-081), cost-budget-report (BB-033's evaluator exists in packages/security/src/resource-controls.ts but wasn't cheap to wire without a new dependency — flagged, not silently skipped), release-coupled-rebuild (BB-070).

Run-health/alerting: generalizes the research worker's run_health.py shape; missed-consecutive-runs and over-budget both raise alerts through BB-034's real SEC-SRC-01/SEC-COST-01 alert policies (buildAlertPayload) — not a new channel. Publish invariant: publish-guard.ts calls straight through to the real assertDiscoveryCannotPublish (BB-039); tested that the two pre-approved public-effect exceptions (link-repair, release-rebuild) are not a bypass route for anything else. Audit correlation: job writes are shaped as real DomainAuditEvent/DomainOutboxMessage objects (commitWithAudit-compatible, verified via a devDependency-only import test) with jobRunId as the correlation id throughout.

infra/gcp/scheduler/scheduled-jobs.json is the declarative Cloud Scheduler mirror (status: 'design', not applied) with its own JSON Schema + an 8-test validation suite.

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session after clearing an unrelated stale packages/domain/dist/ artifact that was blocking a clean tsc build for every domain-touching agent in this wave, not caused by BB-084): pnpm --filter @black-book/config test 72/72 pass, typecheck clean. uv run pytest workers/research 60/60 pass (57 pre-existing + 3 new). node --test infra/gcp/scheduler/scheduled-jobs.test.mjs 8/8 pass.

Disclosed design compromises: (1) backup-verification-daily/restore-drill-quarterly are tagged targetWorker.package='security' even though their scripts live in scripts/backup-restore/, outside all three ADR-007 worker packages — a real, minor ADR-007 conformance gap, flagged for a follow-up bead to migrate that logic into workers/security. (2) Discovery-campaign granularity is 4 representative cadence-class entries, not one row per literal adapter id — a cheap, purely mechanical fan-out once BB-073/075 land.
close_reason: Scheduled-job registry (fail-closed, ADR-007-conformant), run-health/alerting reusing BB-034's real alert policies, publish-invariant enforcement wired to the real BB-039 guard, and audit-correlated job writes delivered. 4/15 roster entries wired to real shipped code; 11 documented stubs for not-yet-built beads. 72+60+8 tests passing.

title: BB-083: Citation integrity and link-rot management
description:
Expansion bead BB-083 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (citation integrity and link-rot management).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("everything possible is cited and linked appropriately, and there is a pattern for updating dead links and at a minimum, marking the link as such but providing a term to help the user search")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-016, BB-019, BB-030

Why: an archive of record lives and dies by its citations, and the web rots — a significant share of cited URLs die within years. The provenance model (BB-016) stores captures; nothing yet detects rot, repairs links, or degrades gracefully for the reader.

Deliver:
- Citation completeness gate: every published claim carries at least one citation with URL (or offline-source designation), source name, capture pointer, and retrieval date — enforced fail-closed at projection build (BB-019), not left to reviewer diligence. Offline/analog sources (books, physical archives, oral interviews) get structured citations without URLs; "unsourced" is not a publishable state.
- Link-health checker (scheduled via BB-084): periodic re-verification of every cited URL through the BB-030 URL-safety fetch policy; classify alive / redirected / changed (content drift vs capture) / dead (404/410/NXDOMAIN/paywalled). Respect robots/rate norms; budget-capped; state machine with retry before declaring death.
- Automatic repair ladder, in order: (1) follow and record permanent redirects; (2) swap to the stored Wayback capture as the primary public link, original kept as "originally published at"; (3) if no capture exists, attempt Save Page Now retroactively; (4) only then mark dead.
- Reader-facing degraded citation (the owner's "Try searching for" pattern): a dead-link citation renders as clearly marked ("Original link unavailable — archived copy" or "link dead as of <date>") plus a generated search suggestion built deterministically from stored citation metadata (source title + author + key named entities from the capture) — e.g. Try searching for: "Rosewood massacre grand jury report 1923 Florida". No LLM required; the suggestion derives from fields we already store.
- Content-drift flag: if a live page's current content no longer matches the capture (hash/fuzzy-similarity check), flag for research review — the citation may now cite something different from what the claim relied on; the capture remains the evidentiary anchor.
- Citation health telemetry: rot rate per source class feeds the source registry's authority signals (a source family whose links rot fast is a durability signal for BB-043), and an operator dashboard tile in the admin console (BB-056 surface).
design: Link rot is the default fate of citations; an archive of record degrades gracefully or loses credibility. The Wayback capture (BB-073/077) is the evidentiary anchor; the live link is a courtesy. The Try-searching-for pattern gives readers a path even when both fail - derived from fields we already store.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Publication-time citation completeness gate, fail-closed at projection build: every published claim has at least one citation with URL or structured offline-source designation, source name, capture pointer, retrieval date. 2. Scheduled link-health sweep (via BB-084) classifying alive/redirected/drifted/dead, fetching through BB-030 policy, budget-capped, with retry-before-death. 3. Repair ladder implemented and tested: permanent-redirect update -> archived-copy swap (original kept as originally-published-at) -> retroactive Save Page Now -> dead-mark. 4. Dead-citation UI: clearly marked with date, archived copy when available, and a deterministic Try-searching-for suggestion generated from stored citation metadata (title + author + key named entities) - no LLM dependency. 5. Content-drift detection (capture vs live hash/similarity) flags research review. 6. Rot-rate telemetry per source class feeds source-registry authority signals and an admin dashboard tile.
notes: Owner brief 2026-07-17: everything cited and linked appropriately; dead links marked at minimum, with a term to help the user search.

title: BB-082: Historic safety and place-context engine
description:
Expansion bead BB-082 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (historic safety and place-context engine).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("think through the scoring for things like the historic safety... consider proximity of historic events, consider crime reporting, consider whatever other data may be relevant and accessible, and how that should be updated")
Plan: plan.md
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-014, BB-017, BB-040, BB-043, BB-050

Why: the gold corpus already names sundown-town candidates as a required category, and the Green Book lineage makes place-safety context core to the mission. But a naive "safety score" is the single most dangerous feature this product could ship — blending historic racial violence with modern crime statistics into one number reproduces redlining. The design below is layered, cited, and deterministic, matching the confidence-engine discipline.

Deliver:
- LAYERED SIGNALS, NEVER ONE OPAQUE NUMBER. Each layer is separately computed, separately cited, separately dated, and shown with its own methodology note:
  1. Documented historic events layer: proximity/density of events already in our corpus plus vetted external datasets — lynching records (EJI / Seguin-Rigby dataset), massacres and race riots, documented displacement (urban renewal, highway construction). Time-banded by era, never decayed to zero — history does not expire.
  2. Sundown-town layer: Tougaloo College Historical Database of Sundown Towns taxonomy preserved verbatim (possible / probable / surely) — their statuses are claims with confidence, exactly our claims model; never flattened to a boolean.
  3. Exclusion-infrastructure layer: HOLC redlining grades (Mapping Inequality, University of Richmond), racially restrictive covenant projects where digitized — structural context, clearly historical.
  4. Presence/affirmation layer (the counterweight — this engine must show life, not only harm): Green Book sites, historic Black churches/schools/business districts, HBCUs, heritage markers from our own corpus.
  5. Modern context layer (handle with extreme care): FBI hate-crime statistics (annual, on-mission) as the primary modern signal. General crime reporting (FBI CDE/NIBRS) only as clearly labeled context with an explicit bias caveat — reported crime measures policing patterns, not safety for Black people; it never feeds any composite. NAACP-style travel advisories recorded as sourced claims when they exist.
- Composite "place context" summary ONLY from layers 1-4, with published weights, versioned methodology (BB-043 pattern: versioned engine, input fingerprints, audited recalculation), and the full layer breakdown one interaction away. Constitution language rules apply: procedural, evidence-capped phrasing; no place is branded "unsafe" — the site presents documented history and lets it speak.
- Every datum cited: source, retrieval date, capture pointer (BB-083 integration); every layer's dataset registered through the BB-037 source registry with rights/attribution metadata (EJI and Tougaloo have specific citation requirements — record them).
- Update cadence per source, wired into BB-084 automation: FBI data annually on release; Tougaloo/Mapping Inequality checked for dataset revisions; our own corpus layers recompute on release activation. Each layer displays "as of" dates.
- Dispute path: place-context layers are claims — the BB-053/BB-055 dispute and correction machinery applies to them like any other claim.
- Map integration (with BB-051/BB-070): layers renderable as opt-in map overlays, following the dignity rules — muted rendering, no red danger shading, narrative off-ramps to the underlying evidence.
design: Datasets: EJI / Seguin-Rigby lynching records; Tougaloo College Historical Database of Sundown Towns; Mapping Inequality HOLC redlining (Univ. of Richmond); digitized covenant projects; Green Book sites + our own corpus for the presence layer; FBI hate-crime statistics (annual) as the primary modern signal; FBI CDE/NIBRS general crime ONLY as labeled context - reported crime measures policing patterns, not safety for Black people, and blending it into a composite reproduces redlining. Language: constitution caps apply; the site presents documented history, it does not brand places unsafe.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Five layers implemented, each separately computed, cited, dated, with its own methodology note: documented-events, sundown-town, exclusion-infrastructure, presence/affirmation, modern-context. 2. Composite place-context summary derives ONLY from layers 1-4 with published weights; a test proves general crime statistics can never enter the composite; the hate-crime layer renders with an explicit bias caveat. 3. Tougaloo taxonomy preserved verbatim (possible/probable/surely - statuses are claims with confidence, never booleans); EJI and Tougaloo citation requirements recorded in the source registry. 4. Versioned methodology engine with input fingerprints and audited recalculation (BB-043 pattern). 5. Per-source update cadences registered in BB-084; as-of dates displayed on every layer. 6. Dispute/correction path works on layer claims like any claim. 7. Map overlays are opt-in and follow the BB-051 dignity rules (muted rendering, no danger shading, narrative off-ramps to evidence).
notes: Owner brief 2026-07-17: thoughtful scoring for historic safety - proximity of historic events, crime reporting, other relevant accessible data, and how it updates. The layered design is the guard against building a redlining machine.

title: BB-077: UGC compliance and living-person ethics layer
description:
Expansion bead BB-077 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (UGC compliance and living-person ethics layer).

Source: crowdsource-ingestion research (legal posture verified against primary sources)
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-003, BB-015, BB-016

Why: the crowdsource lane (BB-073/074/075/076) ingests user-generated and third-party content with per-source legal obligations and living-person risk that the federal-archive adapters never had. This layer is cross-cutting policy — built once, enforced everywhere, fail-closed.

Deliver:
- Evidence-pointer doctrine encoded as policy: store URL + short snippet (1-2 sentences, the minimum needed to judge relevance) + Wayback capture pointer + retrieval metadata; never self-host full page copies; always link out. This is the strongest fair-use posture (search-caching and Google Books precedents; archives protected when viewable portions are minimized).
- Per-source obligations registry (extends BB-016 rights model): machine-readable obligations per source class — Reddit: contractual deletion-sync <= 48h, no republication, no ML training; Brave: storage-rights tier required; RSS/IA/DPLA: attribution + liveness re-checks. Adapters cannot run without an obligations entry (fail-closed, BB-037 pattern).
- Deletion-sync framework: shared scheduler + purge mechanics that BB-074's Reddit job and future adapters plug into; purge cascades through quarantine, graylist, and research-case attachments with audit records (audit keeps the fact of deletion, not the content).
- Living-person ethics above the legal floor (extends BB-015 and the constitution): no cross-source aggregation of a living person's personal details into profiles; elevated verification threshold before any UGC-derived claim about a living person advances (map to the existing 0.9 high-impact confidence tier or add a rule — versioned constitution change); deanonymization prohibited everywhere (contractual for Reddit, policy for all).
- Public takedown/contest process: a reachable page + workflow routing into the corrections pipeline (BB-055) with SLA; CCPA "publicly available" posture documented in docs/security/ so future counsel review starts from written ground truth.
design: Fair-use posture per research: search-caching (Field v Google) and Google Books precedents protect indexing + minimal excerpts that do not substitute for the work; archives strongest when viewable portions are minimized and full-page preservation is delegated to Wayback. CPRA publicly-available exception covers public posts, but Reddit deletion-sync is CONTRACTUAL regardless of privacy law. Ethics layer goes beyond the legal floor for living people.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Per-source obligations registry (extends BB-016 rights model): adapters fail closed without an entry; Reddit, Brave/Exa, RSS, Internet Archive, DPLA entries present. 2. Evidence-pointer doctrine enforced by schema/tests: short snippet (1-2 sentences) + mandatory outbound link + Wayback capture pointer; no self-hosted page copies. 3. Deletion-sync framework: shared scheduler + purge cascading through quarantine, graylist, and case attachments, with audit-of-deletion records (fact of deletion kept, content purged). 4. Living-person UGC rules versioned in the constitution: no cross-source aggregation into profiles; elevated verification threshold for UGC-derived claims about living people (mapping to the 0.9 high-impact tier decided and tested); deanonymization prohibited. 5. Public takedown/contest page + workflow routing into BB-055 corrections with an SLA. 6. CCPA/publicly-available posture documented in docs/security/.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: per-source obligations registry (packages/domain/src/rights/obligations.ts) extending BB-016 rights, mirroring BB-037's fail-closed adapter-registry pattern — Reddit/Brave-Exa/RSS/IA/DPLA entries seeded, unregistered sources rejected. Evidence-pointer doctrine (evidence-pointer.ts) enforced by schema: 320-char/60-word snippet cap, mandatory https URL + Wayback capture pointer, no full-page field, full-page-snippet rejection tested. Deletion-sync framework (deletion-sync.ts): pure planDeletionSyncPurge/applyDeletionSyncPurge cascading quarantine/graylist/research-case-attachment targets, BB-018 audit/outbox-shaped record capturing only the fact of deletion. Living-person UGC ethics (living-person-ugc.ts): assertNoCrossSourceProfileAggregation, assertUgcLivingPersonClaimMayAdvance (maps to the existing 0.9 high-impact confidence tier), assertNoDeanonymization — versioned into the constitution as ugcLivingPersonRules (policyVersion held at 1.0.0, matching BB-015's precedent; TS/JSON/Python all in sync, tested). Takedown request model (takedown.ts) with 72h-ack/30-day-resolution SLA, BB-029-compatible, distinctTag='takedown' — no UI (BB-055's job). docs/security/ugc-compliance-layer.md + ugc-legal-posture.md (CPRA publicly-available exception vs. Reddit's contractual, unconditional deletion-sync).

21 new tests (packages/domain/src/rights.test.ts), all passing. pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test/typecheck clean (one unrelated pre-existing failure in src/similarity/near-duplicate.test.ts belongs to the concurrent BB-071 workstream, not this bead). pnpm --filter @black-book/schemas test/typecheck clean (11/11, including the new ugcLivingPersonRules sync test). packages/constitution Python: uv run pytest 9/9 clean.

Honest mechanical-vs-documented split: fail-closed obligations lookup, evidence-pointer schema caps, deletion-sync content removal, elevated confidence threshold, and deanonymization guard are mechanically enforced (only as strong as callers actually invoking them). 'No cross-source aggregation' is NOT fully mechanically enforceable codebase-wide by one function — assertNoCrossSourceProfileAggregation only catches aggregation routed through it; broader prevention remains a code-review constraint, stated explicitly in the docs.

Known follow-on gap: packages/firebase's StateMutation (audit-outbox.ts) only supports create/set/update, no delete — real Firestore purge wiring needs a delete mutation kind added by whoever builds the live BB-084 scheduler integration or the BB-073-076 adapters.
close_reason: Per-source obligations registry, evidence-pointer doctrine, deletion-sync framework, and versioned living-person UGC ethics rules delivered with 21 passing tests. Full mechanical-vs-documented breakdown and one follow-on gap (Firestore delete mutation) recorded in notes.

title: BB-076: Community lead submission and consensus review
description:
Expansion bead BB-076 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (community lead submission and consensus review).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + crowdsource-ingestion research
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-029, BB-032, BB-077

Why: several high-value channels have no compliant automated path — Facebook Groups (API killed April 2024, CrowdTangle shut down, Meta Content Library is academia-only), Discord, closed forums, family papers, oral memory. The compliant pattern is human submission into a moderated queue. This extends the BB-029 quarantine and BB-055 corrections lane into a general "submit a lead" capability.

Deliver:
- Public "Submit a lead" form: URL or description + why it matters + optional location/era + optional contributor attestation and contact; App Check + rate limits + BB-029 quarantine (create-only, never publicly readable). A bookmarklet/share-target variant is a stretch goal.
- Consensus review engine (Zooniverse/Caesar pattern): each quarantined lead is independently reviewed by N reviewers; agreement threshold routes to auto-advance (becomes a discovery candidate) vs expert review; disagreements never silently resolve. Reviewer agreement becomes a corroboration signal into the BB-043 confidence engine — deterministic, versioned, tested.
- Pending-changes invariant (Wikipedia model): nothing from this lane is visible anywhere public until it has passed review AND the standard research-case pipeline — leads seed research, they never publish.
- Adversarial posture (Queering the Map lesson — a community-memory project on racially charged history WILL be attacked): quarantine-by-default, BB-004 abuse-corpus fixtures must auto-quarantine, spam/flood controls via BB-025.
- Moderator wellbeing runbook: queue design that batches distressing content with warnings, rotation guidance, escalation path — reviewing racist submissions is real emotional labor; plan for it.
design: Zooniverse/Caesar pattern: independent classifications + consensus aggregation + expert gold-mode routing - maps directly onto the confidence pipeline. Wikipedia pending-changes: untrusted edits invisible until trusted review. Queering the Map lesson: a community-memory project on racially charged history WILL receive attack submissions - quarantine-by-default is existential, and moderation is real emotional labor to be designed for.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Submit-a-lead form live behind App Check + rate limits, writing create-only into BB-029 quarantine. 2. Consensus review engine deterministic, versioned, tested: N independent reviews, agreement-threshold routing (auto-advance vs expert review); disagreements never silently resolve. 3. Reviewer agreement feeds BB-043 as a corroboration signal. 4. BB-004 abuse-corpus fixtures auto-quarantine (adversarial submission test). 5. Moderation runbook committed including reviewer-wellbeing guidance (batching, warnings, rotation, escalation). 6. Pending-changes invariant test: nothing from this lane visible publicly until review AND research-case pipeline complete.
notes: Covers channels with no compliant API: Facebook Groups (API dead since 2024, CrowdTangle shut, Meta Content Library academia-only), Discord, closed forums, family papers, oral memory.

title: BB-073: Community discovery adapters wave 1 (RSS, Internet Archive, DPLA, Wayback)
description:
Expansion bead BB-073 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (community discovery adapters, wave 1 — the compliant workhorses).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("there has to be some search framework to crowdsource information... eg reddit, blog posts... I don't want to lose the things that fall through the cracks") + crowdsource-ingestion research
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-030, BB-037, BB-039, BB-077

Why: the adapter fleet covers only Wikimedia and federal archives; community memory (local historical societies, personal blogs, museum news, digitized community collections) is where under-documented history lives, and today the docs treat community content only as an attack vector. This bead adds the compliant, ungated channels; Reddit and web-search discovery are separate beads (BB-074, BB-075) because they carry access gates and cost.

Deliver (all through the BB-037 registry contract: start disabled, approved policy required, canary, drift quarantine, kill switch, provenance stamping — same isolation as BB-045/046):
- RSS/Atom feed adapter + a curated feed registry (historical societies, local museums, library blogs, university digital-collection news, vetted personal blogs). Cheapest and most compliant channel — publishers deliberately syndicate; storing title + link + summary is low-risk. Feed registry is versioned config with add/remove audit.
- Internet Archive adapter: Advanced Search + Scrape API (cursor-based) + Metadata API — no approval gate; high value for digitized Black newspapers, city directories, community uploads.
- DPLA v2 adapter (api.dp.la/v2, free key, ~50M items): abstracted behind the registry contract and monitored — DPLA's aggregation program transitions to Cleveland Public Library starting July 2026; adapter must tolerate endpoint churn.
- Wayback Save Page Now capture layer: every discovered URL is submitted to SPN (authenticated, modest concurrency, retry on 429) and the pointer stored — we keep archive URLs + minimal snippets, not self-hosted page copies (fair-use posture per BB-077).
- Source-tier trust wiring: community_oral / self_published / news_reportage classifications (already in the constitution) map to low authority weights in the relevance (BB-040) and confidence (BB-043) engines; weak signals produce candidates only (BB-038 rule) and can never independently reach include — crowdsourced items SEED research cases, they never publish.
- Graylist recall lane: below-threshold candidates from these adapters are parked with disposition, never silently dropped — queryable for corroboration when new evidence arrives (the "falls through the cracks" safety net).

Constraint: all fetches go through the BB-030 URL-safety/SSRF policy and quarantine queue.
design: Compliant workhorse channels per 2026 research: RSS/Atom (publisher-syndicated, lowest risk); Internet Archive APIs (open, no gate; digitized Black newspapers + city directories); DPLA v2 (free key, ~50M items; aggregation program transitions to Cleveland Public Library July 2026 - adapter must tolerate churn); Wayback SPN as capture layer so we store archive pointers + minimal snippets, not page copies (fair-use posture per BB-077).
acceptance_criteria: 1. RSS/Atom, Internet Archive (Scrape + Metadata), and DPLA v2 adapters land fixtures-first through the BB-037 registry (disabled default, approved policy required, canary, drift quarantine, kill switch, provenance stamping). 2. Wayback Save Page Now capture pointer required on every discovered URL before review eligibility. 3. Source-tier weights wired: community_oral/self_published tiers cannot independently reach include (weak-signal rule holds); crowdsourced items seed research cases and never publish directly. 4. Graylist: below-threshold candidates parked with disposition and queryable for later corroboration - never silently dropped. 5. All fetches through BB-030 URL-safety policy. 6. Feed registry is versioned config with add/remove audit.
notes: Owner brief: crowdsource framework so things that fall through the cracks of formal archives get found. Reddit and web-search are separate beads (BB-074/075) due to gates and cost.

title: BB-072: Hybrid retrieval and ranking fusion
description:
Expansion bead BB-072 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (hybrid retrieval and ranking fusion).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("search... sound, thoughtful, multiple angles, weighs things appropriately, considers relevance") + vector-search research
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-047, BB-049, BB-071

Why: one retrieval angle is never sound. Structured/prefix queries (BB-049) are precise but brittle; vector KNN (BB-071) recalls what keywords miss but can hallucinate relevance. Sound search weighs multiple signals and explains itself.

Deliver:
- Parallel-lane retrieval in api-public: bounded structured/prefix lane + vector KNN lane run concurrently, merged with reciprocal rank fusion (RRF) — cheap, deterministic, no learned ranker.
- Deterministic re-rank on the fused list honoring BB-040 principles: relevance and connection strength over fame; geographic proximity when the query is place-anchored; era match; confidence tier as a tiebreaker never a filter (low-confidence published records still surface, labeled).
- Explanation surface: each result carries a "why this result" affordance (matched name / near-match / place / era / topic) consistent with the public "why this appears" language (BB-054) — numeric scores never exposed publicly (constitution rule).
- Fallback ladder: vector lane unavailable or over-budget => structured-only results, degraded flag in telemetry; both lanes down => release snapshot browse. Kill switch per lane (BB-035 pattern).
- Retrieval evaluation wired into the BB-047 gold-corpus harness: labeled query set (name lookups, misspellings, descriptive queries, place queries), precision/recall/MRR targets recorded before launch; fusion weights are config, changes require re-running the eval (no silent tuning).

Escalation: same ADR-013 triggers as BB-071 (Typesense / Firestore Enterprise before Vertex).
design: Sound search = multiple angles weighed deterministically: structured lane for precision, vector lane for recall, RRF for cheap deterministic fusion (no learned ranker - determinism invariant holds). Matches ADR-008 doctrine: external engines only after measured insufficiency.
acceptance_criteria: 1. RRF fusion of structured + vector lanes behind a flag with per-lane kill switches. 2. Deterministic re-rank honors BB-040 principles (connection strength over fame; era and place match; confidence as tiebreaker never filter); numeric scores never appear in public payloads. 3. Why-this-result affordance on every result consistent with BB-054 language. 4. Fallback ladder tested: vector lane down -> structured-only + degraded telemetry flag; both down -> snapshot browse. 5. Labeled query set (names, misspellings, descriptive, place queries) with precision/recall/MRR gates wired into the BB-047 harness; fusion weight changes require re-running the eval.
notes: Owner brief: search must be sound, thoughtful, consider multiple angles and elements, weigh things appropriately, consider relevance.

title: BB-071: Embedding pipeline and Firestore vector search
description:
Expansion bead BB-071 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (semantic search inside Firebase).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + vector-search research (citations in Design notes)
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-019, BB-026, BB-047

Why: ADR-008/ADR-011 search is bounded structured/prefix Firestore queries — sound for precision, but it cannot find what users describe in their own words ("the school where the sit-ins started") and gives the research pipeline no recall lane for near-matches. Firestore native KNN vector search is GA and stays entirely inside Firebase, resolving the owner question "idk how vector would work with firebase or within firebase."

Deliver:
- Embedding pipeline owned by us (NOT the pre-GA firestore-vector-search extension — we need control over model, dims, retries): on projection write/release build, embed title + summary + place/era context with gemini-embedding-001, truncated to 768 dims (Matryoshka; Firestore cap is 2048), unit-normalized, DOT_PRODUCT distance. Cost note: embedding 100k docs of ~500 tokens is roughly $7.50 one-time — negligible.
- Firestore vector indexes: composite vector indexes with pre-filters (state, entity kind, era bucket) because Firestore KNN is exact/flat scan — pre-filtering is the lever that keeps scans small and p95 sane. Budget for the 10x read metering on vector index entries (1 read per 100 entries vs per 1000).
- Server-only KNN: find_nearest exposed ONLY through api-public server code (web/mobile SDKs don't support it anyway), behind BB-026 guardrails — capped limit (<= existing page caps, max 1000 neighbors is the platform ceiling), distance threshold, App Check, rate limits, kill switch.
- Research-side reuse (internal environment): the same embeddings power candidate recall ("find sources similar to this accepted one") and near-duplicate detection in the discovery pipeline — a recall safety net so weak-signal items surface for corroboration instead of falling through the cracks.
- Explicit non-goals with recorded rationale: Vertex AI Vector Search rejected at this scale (always-on node floor ~$68+/mo/node, realistically several hundred/mo, unjustified under ~1M vectors); external engines (Typesense/Algolia) deferred per ADR-008 doctrine until measured insufficiency.

Escalation triggers (record in ADR-013 search evolution): corpus > ~1M vectors in query scope, sustained p95 KNN latency failures despite pre-filtering, or keyword-UX needs (typo tolerance, facets) → evaluate Typesense extension or Firestore Enterprise (native full-text + vector pipelines) before Vertex.
design: Firestore native KNN is GA: flat/exact index, 2048-dim cap, pre-filtering via composite indexes, max 1000 neighbors, server SDKs only, no snapshot listeners. Cost: 1 read per 100 vector index entries (10x normal metering) + 1 per doc returned - pre-filtering is the p95 lever. Embedding cost negligible (about 7.50 USD per 100k docs at 500 tokens). Vertex AI Vector Search unjustified under ~1M vectors (roughly 68+ USD/month/node floor, realistically several hundred). Escalation order when triggers fire: Typesense extension or Firestore Enterprise (native full-text + vector pipelines) BEFORE Vertex.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Embedding pipeline in our own code (pre-GA extension rejected): gemini-embedding-001 truncated to 768 dims, unit-normalized, DOT_PRODUCT; on-write + backfill paths. 2. Composite vector indexes with state/entity-kind/era pre-filters deployed. 3. find_nearest served only via api-public server path behind BB-026 guardrails, App Check, rate limits, kill switch; capped limits and distance thresholds. 4. Research-side reuse: candidate recall + near-duplicate detection in discovery. 5. Gold-corpus retrieval eval passing with recorded recall targets. 6. ADR-013 records the Vertex AI Vector Search rejection rationale (always-on node cost floor) and escalation triggers.
notes: Resolves owner question re vector within Firebase: stays fully inside Firestore. Sequenced after design + map waves per owner priority.

title: BB-081: Relevance and discovery feedback loop
description:
Expansion bead BB-081 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (relevance and discovery feedback loop).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("weighs things appropriately, considers relevance") + pipeline review
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-040, BB-044, BB-047

Why: the relevance and confidence engines are deterministic and versioned — good — but nothing closes the loop: researcher accept/reject/override decisions are recorded in case history and never systematically compared against what the engines predicted. Without this, weights fossilize and the graylist recall lane never proves whether it catches what matters.

Deliver:
- Decision log: every research-case transition that accepts, rejects, or overrides an engine verdict (BB-044 already has append-only history) is extracted into a calibration dataset — engine scores at decision time vs human disposition, with the input fingerprints BB-043 already stamps.
- Periodic recalibration report (scheduled via BB-084): per-dimension disagreement analysis (which relevance dimensions most often disagree with humans), query-pack effectiveness (BB-038 metrics: which packs produce accepted candidates vs noise), graylist yield (how many parked weak signals were later corroborated — the falls-through-the-cracks metric), and source-tier precision by adapter.
- Human-approved weight changes ONLY: the report proposes; changes ship as versioned policy/config updates (constitution versioning pattern) and must pass the BB-047 gold-corpus gates before taking effect. No silent auto-tuning, no learned ranker — the determinism invariant holds.
- Drift alarms: if live human-vs-engine disagreement exceeds a threshold for a period, raise an operator alert (BB-034 pattern) — the calibration equivalent of schema drift quarantine.
design: The engines are deterministic and versioned but nothing closes the loop today - weights fossilize and the graylist never proves its worth. This is the calibration equivalent of schema-drift quarantine.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Decision log extracted from BB-044 case history: engine scores at decision time vs human disposition, keyed by the BB-043 input fingerprints. 2. Scheduled recalibration report (via BB-084): per-dimension disagreement, query-pack effectiveness, graylist yield (parked weak signals later corroborated - the falls-through-the-cracks metric), source-tier precision by adapter. 3. Weight changes ship ONLY as versioned policy/config updates that pass the BB-047 gold-corpus gates before effect (test proves the config path; no silent tuning, no learned ranker). 4. Drift alarm: sustained human-vs-engine disagreement above threshold raises an operator alert (BB-034 pattern).
notes: Determinism invariant holds: the report proposes, humans approve, gold corpus gates.

title: BB-075: Web-search and Common Crawl discovery
description:
Expansion bead BB-075 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (web-search and Common Crawl discovery).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + crowdsource-ingestion research (API landscape verified 2026)
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-033, BB-037, BB-073, BB-077

Why: RSS and archive APIs only find sources we already know to watch. Programmatic web search finds the obscure personal blog or church-history page nobody registered. The 2026 landscape is narrow and must be chosen deliberately: Bing Search API retired Aug 2025; Google Programmable Search JSON API is closed to new customers and sunsets Jan 2027 — do not build on either.

Deliver:
- Primary adapter: Brave Search API on the storage-rights plan (base-plan ToS does NOT grant result storage; the storage-rights tier does — get the storage terms confirmed in writing before persisting anything, ~$5/1k queries) OR Exa semantic search (~$7/1k, zero-data-retention posture, strong at "obscure page" discovery; confirm storage terms with sales). Decision recorded on this bead with the confirmation evidence.
- Common Crawl retrospective sweeps (free, AWS Open Data + Hugging Face): CDX-index queries for name/place mentions across historical crawls — the retrospective complement to real-time search; research/fair-use terms match our posture.
- Query generation from BB-038 query packs (period-appropriate terms, researchOnlyOffensive terms never sent to external APIs — check this constraint explicitly), geographically seeded per state/county.
- Budget guards via BB-033 cost controls: per-campaign query budgets, monthly spend caps, alerts; every external query and result stamped with provenance (API, query, timestamp, plan/terms version).
- Results flow into the standard candidate pipeline (BB-039 dedup, BB-040 relevance, graylist for weak signals); discovered URLs go through BB-030 URL safety and the BB-073 Wayback capture layer.
design: 2026 landscape: Bing Search API retired Aug 2025; Google Programmable Search closed to new customers, sunsets Jan 2027 - build on neither. Brave ~5 USD/1k queries but base ToS does NOT grant storage; the storage-rights tier does. Exa ~7 USD/1k, semantic, strong at obscure-page discovery, zero-data-retention posture; confirm storage terms in writing. Common Crawl free (AWS Open Data + Hugging Face), CDX-queryable, research/fair-use terms - the retrospective complement.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Provider decision recorded WITH written storage-rights confirmation before any result is persisted (Brave storage-rights plan or Exa; evidence attached to this bead). 2. Common Crawl CDX sweep adapter with fixtures. 3. Query generation from BB-038 packs; researchOnlyOffensive terms never sent to external APIs (tested). 4. BB-033 budget caps and alerts enforced per campaign. 5. Provenance stamped on every external query and result (API, query, timestamp, plan/terms version). 6. Discovered URLs flow through BB-030 safety and the BB-073 capture layer into the standard candidate pipeline.
notes: Finds the obscure personal blog or church-history page nobody registered in a feed list.

title: BB-074: Reddit discovery adapter (gated channel)
description:
Expansion bead BB-074 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (Reddit discovery adapter — gated channel).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + crowdsource-ingestion research (Reddit terms verified against primary sources)
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-037, BB-073, BB-077

Why: Reddit threads (r/AskHistorians, r/BlackHistory, city and state subs) surface leads no archive holds — family histories, neighborhood memory, photo identifications. But Reddit access is now gated and contractually constrained, so this adapter is compliance-first by design.

Access reality (2026): self-service API registration closed under Reddit's Responsible Builder Policy — approval is a ticket queue with a 2-4 week reported lead time; unauthenticated .json endpoints return 403 since May 2026. Free tier: 100 queries/min OAuth, non-commercial use. Reddit for Researchers requires a university-affiliated PI (cleaner dataset path if a partnership exists).

Deliver:
- HUMAN STEP (blocks everything else): submit the Responsible Builder application stating the non-commercial archival-research use case plainly; record the outcome on this bead.
- Polling adapter (registry-governed per BB-037): /new listings for a curated, versioned subreddit list — well within 100 QPM. Structural limits to design around: no date-range search, no comment search, 1000-item listing caps.
- Pointer-first storage: permalink, post ID, subreddit, timestamp, author handle (never resolved to identity), plus a minimal working snippet for reviewer triage only.
- Deletion-sync job (contractual, not optional): scheduled liveness re-check of every stored Reddit pointer; content deleted by the user or Reddit is purged from our stores within 48 hours, including snippets — "even if disassociated, de-identified or anonymized" retention violates the terms. Re-verify liveness immediately before any human review or research-case attachment.
- Hard prohibitions encoded as fail-closed tests: no republication of Reddit content anywhere public (threads are cited as discovery leads that pointed researchers to verifiable primary sources); no ML training on Reddit content; no deanonymization of users.

Constraint: adapter ships fixtures-first and stays in canary until the application is approved; if the application is denied, close this bead with reason and route Reddit leads through the BB-076 manual submission lane instead.
design: Reddit 2026 reality (primary sources): self-service API registration closed under Responsible Builder Policy (ticket queue, 2-4 week lead); unauthenticated .json endpoints return 403 since May 2026; free tier 100 QPM non-commercial; deleted-content retention violates terms even if de-identified (48h purge recommended); structural gaps: no date-range search, no comment search, 1000-item listing caps. Reddit for Researchers needs a university PI - cleaner path if a partnership exists.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Responsible Builder application submitted with the non-commercial archival-research use case; outcome recorded on this bead (HUMAN STEP, blocks the rest). 2. Polling adapter respects the 100 QPM free tier; curated subreddit list is versioned config. 3. Pointer-first storage (permalink, IDs, timestamp, minimal triage snippet; author never resolved to identity). 4. Deletion-sync job purges deleted content within 48 hours including snippets; liveness re-checked immediately before any human review or case attachment. 5. Fail-closed tests: no republication anywhere public, no ML training on Reddit content, no deanonymization. 6. Fixtures-first canary until approval; if denied, close with reason and route Reddit leads through BB-076.
notes: Threads are discovery leads pointing researchers to verifiable primary sources - never evidence of record, never republished.

Second adversarial pass in progress: fact registry (BB-086), legal corpus + free-source change monitoring (BB-087), editorial trust surfaces (BB-088), resilience red-team (BB-089) — protection framed as rigor/trust, never defensiveness.

Owner operating principle (2026-07-17): this project gets limited time and money — it must mostly run itself. Binding implications: (1) free tiers and free data sources first, paid only with explicit owner sign-off; (2) every recurring job is budget-capped with a kill switch and fails quiet-but-visible (review queue + digest, never pages); (3) prefer one-time setup over recurring labor; (4) automation proposes, owner disposes in periodic sessions — review queues must be batchable in minutes; (5) scope beads lean — reuse existing packages/models before building parallel systems; (6) when a bead offers a gold-plated vs good-enough path, take good-enough and record the upgrade trigger.

Owner directive 2026-07-17: 'Everything will be in Firebase for now.' Binding: application infra + data + all ingestion (legal APIs, discovery adapters, fact registry) stay Firebase/GCP-native — no multi-cloud data architecture, no required external dependency to ship. The red-team's Cloudflare-in-front (DNS/edge/origin-hiding) and warm off-Google backup are recorded in BB-089 as OPTIONAL hardening with explicit trigger conditions (a takedown/DDoS event, or a high-profile launch), NOT launch-blocking work. Firebase-native protections are primary: App Check + budget kill-switch + degraded snapshot mode (BB-033/035) are the true availability backstop; Firestore PITR/backup (BB-020). Registrar/DNS choice and root-account 2FA are not 'leaving Firebase' — they're account/domain hygiene and stay pre-launch. Reconciles with earlier 'id like to stay in firebase, you have full judgement.'
